summary: Small ftCommon Attack100 codegen fix that refactored CaptureDamageLw physics helpers into static inline bodies plus public wrappers. The change removed a local dont_inline pragma block, added PAD_STACK(4) for fn_800DC624, adjusted Fighter pointer reloads/local variable usage, and produced decomp.dev-reported 100% matches for fn_800DC624, ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys, and ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Coll.

key_files.role: Only changed file; refactored ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys and fn_800DC624 matching-sensitive code.

what_changed: Removed the #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop region around ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_IASA, ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys, and fn_800DC624.
Split ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys into static inline ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys_inline plus a non-inline public ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys wrapper that calls it.
Split fn_800DC624 into static inline fn_800DC624_inline plus a public fn_800DC624 wrapper.
Added PAD_STACK(4) to the public fn_800DC624 wrapper before calling fn_800DC624_inline.

Changed local variable strategy in ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys: uses gobj directly, introduces Fighter* fp for the main state transition/victim pointer, reloads GET_FIGHTER(gobj) into fp2 for the x2226_b2 check, and preserves a Vec3* pos temporary for HSD_JObjSetTranslate.

Changed local variable strategy in fn_800DC624_inline: uses gobj directly, keeps Fighter* temp_r31, and passes &temp_r31->cur_pos directly to HSD_JObjSetTranslate instead of using a Vec3* pos temporary.

decomp.dev reported 3 new matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100: fn_800DC624 (+200 bytes, 33.64% to 100.00%), ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys (+185 bytes, 41.59% to 100.00%), and ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Coll (+20 bytes, 65.86% to 100.00%).
smart_moves: Used static inline helper bodies while keeping the original exported function symbols as wrappers, allowing codegen control without renaming the public API.
Replaced broad dont_inline pragma control with more targeted inline/wrapper structure, reducing collateral effects on adjacent functions.
Added a small explicit PAD_STACK(4) only where needed for fn_800DC624 wrapper matching.
Preserved matching-sensitive redundant GET_FIGHTER behavior by reloading into fp2 before checking x2226_b2 instead of over-optimizing through the earlier Fighter*.
Kept source behavior stable while changing only code structure and temporaries to improve matching.
decomp_lessons: For stubborn fighter action functions, a static inline implementation plus a trivial exported wrapper can be a cleaner matching tactic than a dont_inline pragma block.
Do not assume fewer temporaries is better: matching may require a specific pointer reload, as shown by the separate fp2 GET_FIGHTER(gobj) before x2226_b2.
Stack padding in a wrapper can be enough to recover a target function match without disturbing the inline body shared with nearby logic.

Adjacent function match status can change from pragma/inline decisions even when that adjacent function's visible body barely changes; here ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Coll also reached 100% according to the bot report.
When refactoring for match, keep original side-effect order around Fighter_ChangeMotionState, fn_800DB5D8, fn_800DAA40, ft_80083C00, and HSD_JObjSetTranslate.
naming_conventions: Static inline helper names used the original function name plus an _inline suffix: ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys_inline and fn_800DC624_inline.
Original externally referenced function names were preserved as wrappers: ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys and fn_800DC624.

Local fighter pointer naming mixed descriptive fp/fp2 in ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys_inline with temp_r31 retained in fn_800DC624_inline, suggesting names were chosen for codegen/matching rather than purely for style.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed #pragma dont_inline and controlled inlining explicitly with static inline helper functions.
Introduced wrapper functions around inline bodies to preserve symbol boundaries while influencing generated code.
Used PAD_STACK(4) in fn_800DC624 to match stack layout.
Used direct gobj argument references instead of assigning temp_r30 = gobj.
Forced a second GET_FIGHTER(gobj) reload before the x2226_b2 branch in ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys_inline.
Kept a Vec3* pos temporary in ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys_inline but removed it in fn_800DC624_inline, showing per-function temporary choices mattered.

review_feedback.feedback: Matched code improved by +0.02% / +672 bytes for GALE01, with 3 new 100% matches in ftCo_Attack100.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find other ftCommon functions still using #pragma dont_inline where a static inline body plus wrapper may improve matching.
Search for PAD_STACK in fighter code to compare wrapper stack-padding patterns.
Review nearby CaptureDamageHi/Lw helper functions for duplicated state-transition code that may need the same GET_FIGHTER reload pattern.
Check whether ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Coll's new match was caused by pragma boundary changes rather than direct body edits.
